title: lifecycle-result
description: One canonical, machine-readable record for a single candidate's trip through the experiment lifecycle (preflight -> apply -> measure -> score -> replay -> record). GEPA, materialization, behavioral eval, replay, and promotion map INTO this shape; none invent competing truth fields. authoritative_stage + authoritative_verdict are the single truth a reader/automation should consult (Decision 0004 / Phase 10H0: the measured materialization and broker-mediated behavioral verdict are authoritative; the deterministic shadow scoreboard is advisory). status describes the RUN (a missing real-LM backend is skipped/failed, never a fake allow). Promotes nothing.

status.description: the run's outcome; a missing/unavailable real-LM behavioral backend is skipped/failed and never authoritative_verdict==allow.

stages.description: per-stage detail mapped from the existing tools; a null stage means it was not reached (e.g. behavioral is null when materialization/scope blocked first).

authoritative_stage.description: which stage produced the authoritative verdict. Precedence: materialization-block > off-scope > behavioral > shadow; unavailable when a real-LM backend was required but missing.

authoritative_verdict.description: the single verdict to read. allow requires a behavioral allow (broker-owned, FDR 0, strict 3/3) AND a materialized allow; the shadow scoreboard alone never produces allow.

promotable.description: true only with an authoritative allow + clean redaction + a real qualified backend; PR-only and human-merged regardless.

artifacts.description: repo-relative links to the underlying evidence (the lifecycle-result references them, it does not duplicate their bytes).
